title: rinorea verticillata
intro: Rinorea verticillata, also known as the round-leaved dinkey bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It is a shrub native to Australia, where it is found in the tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and saponins. Rinorea verticillata can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin infections, and respiratory conditions.
